item.title: NLP Series [Part 2] · Word2Vec-Skip-Gram-Negative-Sampling
item.description: from collections import Counter from random import sample import re from typing import List, Tuple import numpy as np import requests from sklearn.metrics.pairwise import cosine_similarity import torch import torch.nn as nn from torch.utils.data import DataLoader, Dataset Most of the steps are similar to our notebook on Word2Vec without negative sampling. The key changes happen with the dataset where we provide a list of indices for negative sample for each forward pass as well as the loss function.

item.title: NLP Series [Part 1] · Word2Vec-Skip-Gram
item.description: from collections import Counter import re from typing import List import numpy as np import requests from sklearn.metrics.pairwise import cosine_similarity import torch import torch.nn as nn from torch.utils.data import DataLoader, Dataset For detailed intro to Word2Vec and word vectors in general, check out these awesome resources: Stanford 2021 nlp lecture Yandex nlp course In this notebook, we will train a Word2Vec skip-gram model from scratch on some text data and manually inspect the trained embeddings at the end.
